articles.headline: Government Launches BharatNet Phase III for Universal Broadband Connectivity Across India
articles.explainer:
• Aims to connect all remaining gram panchayats and villages with high-speed optical fibre internet.
• Utilizes a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model to accelerate deployment and ensure sustainability.
• Expected to boost digital economy, e-governance, telemedicine, and online education in rural areas.
• Builds upon the previous phases of the BharatNet project, addressing last-mile connectivity challenges.
• Focuses on creating an accessible and affordable digital infrastructure backbone for rural India.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment Models
ICT development and its applications
prelimsPoints: BharatNet Phase III launched
Objective: Universal broadband connectivity to gram panchayats and villages
Model: Public-Private Partnership (PPP)
Implementing Ministry: Department of Telecommunications (under Ministry of Communications)
mainsPoints: Significance of digital infrastructure for rural development and economic growth.
Challenges in last-mile connectivity and how the PPP model addresses them.
Impact on e-governance, education, healthcare, and financial inclusion in rural areas.
Role of government initiatives in bridging the digital divide and promoting inclusive growth.
Potential for job creation and local entrepreneurship through enhanced connectivity.
articles.imageDescription: Optical fibre cables being laid in a rural Indian landscape.
articles.trivia: The BharatNet project is one of the largest rural broadband projects in the world.

articles.headline: Nine Injured in Grenade Attack on Srinagar Market; Security Forces Launch Search Operation
articles.explainer:
• A grenade was lobbed by unknown assailants at a busy market in Srinagar, injuring nine civilians.
• Security forces have cordoned off the area and launched a massive search and cordon operation (CASO).
• The attack comes amidst heightened security arrangements in the region, raising concerns.
• Authorities condemned the act as a cowardly attack and vowed to bring the perpetrators to justice.
• The incident highlights ongoing security challenges and the need for continued vigilance in Jammu & Kashmir.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Location: Srinagar, Jammu & Kashmir
Nature of incident: Grenade attack on a market
Impact: Nine civilians injured
Response: Cordon and Search Operation (CASO) by security forces
mainsPoints: Analysis of the evolving security situation in Jammu & Kashmir and persistent threats.
Challenges faced by security forces in preventing such attacks in civilian-dense areas.
Role of intelligence gathering, community policing, and technology in counter-terrorism efforts.
Impact of such incidents on local populace, tourism, and economic activity in the region.
Government's multi-pronged approach to address militancy, maintain peace, and foster development.
articles.imageDescription: Security forces personnel cordoning off a market area in Srinagar.
articles.trivia: Srinagar is historically known for its beautiful Mughal gardens and houseboats on Dal Lake.

articles.headline: RBI MPC Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate, Reaffirms Focus on Inflation Management
articles.explainer:
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its latest meeting.
• Decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level, continuing a cautious monetary policy stance.
• Emphasized vigilance on the inflation trajectory, citing global uncertainties and domestic food price pressures.
• Reiterated commitment to supporting economic growth while primarily ensuring price stability.
• Market analysts anticipate a prolonged pause in rate adjustments, monitoring economic indicators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC meeting outcome: Status quo on repo rate
Primary focus: Inflation management within the target range
Secondary focus: Supporting economic growth
Key monetary policy tool: Repo Rate (rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks)
mainsPoints: Role of RBI's monetary policy in economic stabilization and achieving macro-economic objectives.
Challenges in balancing inflation control and promoting economic growth in a dynamic global environment.
Impact of global economic factors (e.g., crude oil prices, supply chain disruptions) on India's monetary policy decisions.
Effect of an unchanged repo rate on lending, borrowing, investment, and consumer demand.
Significance of the MPC's forward guidance for market expectations and investment climate.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade with its logo.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in India in 2016.

articles.headline: India and France Conclude Strategic Dialogue, Deepen Cooperation in Defence and Indo-Pacific
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue was held, co-chaired by National Security Advisors of both nations.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defence collaboration, including co-development and co-production of military hardware.
• Both sides reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, emphasizing maritime security.
• Explored avenues for cooperation in counter-terrorism, cyber security, and space technology applications.
• The dialogue underscores the robust and growing strategic partnership between India and France.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Dialogue: India-France Strategic Dialogue
Co-chairs: National Security Advisors (NSAs)
Key areas of cooperation: Defence (co-development, co-production), Indo-Pacific, Cyber Security, Space
Nature of relationship: Robust Strategic Partnership
mainsPoints: Significance of India-France strategic partnership in the evolving global order and multipolar world.
Role of France as a key European partner for India in defence technology transfer and strategic autonomy.
Shared vision for a stable, secure, and prosperous Indo-Pacific region and maritime security challenges.
Challenges and opportunities in expanding bilateral cooperation across various sectors.
Impact of such dialogues on India's geopolitical standing and foreign policy objectives, especially in Europe.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and France displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic partnership with India in 1998.

articles.headline: India Announces Enhanced Emission Reduction Targets Ahead of COP29, Reaffirms Climate Leadership
articles.explainer:
• India declared updated Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) with more ambitious emission reduction goals.
• The announcement comes in preparation for the upcoming COP29 summit, showcasing India's commitment to global climate action.
• New targets include increased renewable energy capacity and further reductions in emissions intensity of its economy.
• Emphasizes the principle of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC).
• Aims to balance rapid economic development with environmental sustainability, promoting green growth strategies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: India's new commitment: Enhanced Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs)
Context: Ahead of COP29 (Conference of Parties 29)
Key aspects: Increased renewable energy targets, further reduced emissions intensity
Principle upheld: Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
mainsPoints: India's evolving stance on climate change and its role as a responsible global actor and a voice for developing nations.
Challenges and opportunities in achieving ambitious emission targets while ensuring energy security and economic growth.
Significance of technology transfer, climate finance, and capacity building for developing nations like India.
Impact of climate policies and green growth strategies on various sectors of the Indian economy.
Critique of global climate governance and the imperative for equitable burden sharing among nations.
articles.imageDescription: Solar panels against a blue sky, symbolizing renewable energy.
articles.trivia: COP29 is scheduled to be held in Baku, Azerbaijan.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Advanced Propulsion System for Gaganyaan Human Spaceflight Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) conducted a critical test of an advanced propulsion system.
• The system is designed for the service module of the Gaganyaan mission, India's maiden human spaceflight program.
• The test validated its performance, reliability, and re-ignition capabilities in simulated space conditions.
• Marks a significant milestone in ensuring the safety and success of the crewed mission's orbital phase.
• Further strengthens India's capabilities in independent human spaceflight endeavors and advanced rocketry.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Agency: Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)
Mission: Gaganyaan (India's first human spaceflight mission)
Test: Advanced Propulsion System for the Service Module
Significance: Critical step towards achieving indigenous human spaceflight capability
mainsPoints: Strategic importance of India's human spaceflight program (Gaganyaan) for national prestige and technological advancement.
Technological challenges involved in developing advanced propulsion systems for crewed missions and ensuring astronaut safety.
Potential spin-off benefits of space technology for civilian applications and economic development.
India's position in the global space race and potential for international collaboration in human space exploration.
Economic and scientific impact of indigenous space capabilities on research, innovation, and job creation.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of the Gaganyaan crew module in orbit.
articles.trivia: The Gaganyaan mission aims to send a three-member crew to an orbit of 400 km for a 3-day mission.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds EWS Reservation, Clarifies Application of 'Creamy Layer' Principle
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment affirming the constitutional validity of the 10% EWS reservation.
• Also provided clarity on the application of the 'creamy layer' principle within the EWS category, if any, for future policy.
• The verdict addresses long-standing legal challenges to the reservation for Economically Weaker Sections in admissions and employment.
• Emphasizes the state's power under Articles 15(6) and 16(6) to make special provisions for the advancement of any economically weaker section.
• Expected to have significant implications for social justice discourse and public policy on affirmative action.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Judgment by: Supreme Court of India
Topic: Constitutional validity of 10% EWS (Economically Weaker Sections) reservation
Outcome: Upheld the validity of EWS reservation
Additional clarification: Application of 'creamy layer' principle for EWS category
mainsPoints: Constitutional basis and evolution of reservations in India, including the departure with EWS from caste-based criteria.
Arguments for and against EWS reservation, considering principles of equality, meritocracy, and economic disadvantage.
Debate around the 'creamy layer' concept and its applicability across different reservation categories and socio-economic groups.
Impact of the judgment on social equity, access to opportunities in education and employment, and the broader reservation policy.
Role of the judiciary in interpreting constitutional provisions related to affirmative action and balancing competing interests.
articles.trivia: The 10% EWS reservation was introduced by the 103rd Constitutional Amendment Act in 2019.

articles.headline: Ministry of Education Conducts High-Level Review of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive meeting was held to assess the progress and challenges in implementing NEP 2020 across states.
• Discussions focused on curriculum reforms, digital education initiatives, and teacher training programs.
• Stakeholders emphasized the need for greater synergy and coordination between central and state governments for effective rollout.
• Identified key areas requiring accelerated action, including early childhood care and education (ECCE) and foundational literacy.
• NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet 21st-century demands and foster holistic development.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
mainsPoints: Salient features and transformative objectives of NEP 2020, including its vision for holistic and multidisciplinary education.
Challenges in implementing large-scale education reforms across a diverse and federal nation, involving multiple stakeholders.
Role of technology in transforming education delivery, access, and learning outcomes, especially in remote areas.
Impact of NEP on skill development, employability, vocational education, and fostering critical thinking among students.
Recommendations for effective coordination between different levels of government and educational institutions for successful implementation.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a classroom engaging with digital learning tools.
articles.trivia: NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India since independence, following policies in 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: Economic Survey Projects Robust Q2 GDP Growth, Manufacturing Sector Shows Strong Recovery
articles.explainer:
• Latest economic survey data indicates a strong rebound in India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) for the second quarter.
• The manufacturing sector, in particular, has shown significant resilience and growth, driving overall economic expansion.
• Positive sentiment attributed to government's structural reforms, robust domestic demand, and investment push.
• Challenges remain from global headwinds, geopolitical uncertainties, and inflationary pressures, requiring continued policy vigilance.
• Projections suggest India is on track to be one of the fastest-growing major economies globally, sustaining momentum.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Q2 GDP growth projected as robust by economic survey
Key driver for growth: Manufacturing sector showing strong recovery
Contributing factors: Government's structural reforms, strong domestic demand
Lingering challenges: Global headwinds, inflationary pressures
mainsPoints: Analysis of factors contributing to India's economic growth momentum post-pandemic and global shocks.
Role of the manufacturing sector in job creation, value addition, and achieving 'Make in India' objectives.
Government policies and initiatives (e.g., PLI schemes) aimed at boosting industrial output and investment.
Impact of global economic slowdowns, supply chain disruptions, and geopolitical tensions on India's growth trajectory.
Strategies for achieving sustainable, inclusive, and resilient economic growth in the long term.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing an upward trending economic growth chart.
articles.trivia: India's economy is often characterized by its large domestic market and youthful population.

articles.headline: Government Issues New Guidelines to Strengthen Cyber Security Framework for Critical Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released updated guidelines for critical infrastructure sectors.
• Aims to enhance the resilience of sectors like energy, finance, transport, and communication against cyber threats.
• Mandates regular security audits, threat intelligence sharing, and establishment of dedicated cyber security cells.
• Focuses on a 'defense-in-depth' strategy to protect essential services from sophisticated cyber attacks and espionage.
• Part of India's broader national cyber security strategy to safeguard digital assets and ensure national security.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Issuing authority for guidelines: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
Purpose: Strengthen cyber security for Critical Information Infrastructure (CII)
Key measures mandated: Regular security audits, threat intelligence sharing, dedicated cyber security cells
Strategy adopted: 'Defense-in-depth' approach
mainsPoints: Growing threat of cyber attacks on critical infrastructure and its implications for national security and economic stability.
Challenges in securing diverse, interconnected, and often legacy critical systems across various sectors.
Government's multi-stakeholder approach to cyber security governance, involving public and private entities.
Role of international cooperation and information sharing in combating trans-national cyber threats and state-sponsored attacks.
Balancing security imperatives with innovation, technological advancement, and individual privacy in the digital domain.
articles.imageDescription: A digital representation of interconnected networks and security shields.
articles.trivia: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.

articles.headline: India Hosts BIMSTEC Senior Officials' Meeting, Emphasizes Regional Connectivity and Trade
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the Senior Officials' Meeting (SOM) of the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC).
• Deliberations focused on enhancing regional connectivity, trade facilitation, and people-to-people exchanges.
• Member states discussed progress on various cooperation sectors, including energy, disaster management, and agriculture.
• Reaffirmed commitment to fostering a prosperous, integrated, and secure Bay of Bengal region.
• BIMSTEC is crucial for India's 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies, bridging South and Southeast Asia.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
India and its neighborhood- relations.
prelimsPoints: Meeting: BIMSTEC Senior Officials' Meeting (SOM)
Host: India
Focus areas: Regional connectivity, trade facilitation, energy, disaster management, agriculture
Significance for India: Integration with 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies
mainsPoints: Strategic importance of BIMSTEC for India's regional engagement, economic integration, and geopolitical objectives.
Challenges and opportunities in promoting economic integration and connectivity in the Bay of Bengal region.
Comparison of BIMSTEC with other regional groupings like SAARC, highlighting its unique advantages and focus.
Role of India in driving BIMSTEC's agenda, capacity building, and project implementation.
Potential for BIMSTEC to address common challenges like climate change, disaster management, and maritime security.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of BIMSTEC member countries displayed together.
articles.trivia: BIMSTEC comprises seven member states: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand.

articles.headline: Government Declares New Ramsar Sites, Bolstering India's Wetland Conservation Efforts
articles.explainer:
• Several new wetlands across India have been designated as Ramsar Sites, significantly increasing the total count.
• This declaration signifies their international importance for biodiversity, ecological functions, and hydrological services.
• The move underscores India's commitment to the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands of International Importance.
• Aims to promote conservation and wise use of these critical ecosystems, which are vital for environmental health.
• Wetlands play a crucial role in water purification, flood control, carbon sequestration, and supporting diverse flora and fauna.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Environmental conservation; national parks, wildlife sanctuaries, biosphere reserves, tiger reserves, Ramsar sites, etc.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Declaration: New Ramsar Sites in India
Purpose: Enhanced wetland conservation and recognition of international importance
International convention involved: Ramsar Convention on Wetlands
Role of wetlands: Water purification, flood control, carbon sequestration, biodiversity support
mainsPoints: Ecological significance of wetlands, including their role as biodiversity hotspots and providers of ecosystem services.
Objectives and implementation of the Ramsar Convention in India, and the process of designating Ramsar Sites.
Challenges in managing and protecting Ramsar Sites, including anthropogenic pressures, pollution, and climate change impacts.
Government initiatives and policies for wetland conservation beyond Ramsar designation, such as the National Wetland Conservation Programme.
Role of local communities, scientific research, and public awareness in effective wetland management and sustainable use.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds.
articles.trivia: The Ramsar Convention is the only global treaty focused on a single ecosystem type – wetlands.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Standing Committee Recommends Comprehensive Reforms in Electoral Funding
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report on electoral reforms, with a focus on funding transparency.
• Recommended measures to enhance accountability and reduce the influence of black money in elections.
• Suggestions include strengthening the Election Commission's powers and regulating political advertising more strictly.
• Emphasized the need for public disclosure of donor details and stricter enforcement of existing laws.
• Aims to foster a more equitable, transparent, and democratic electoral process in India.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Body: Parliamentary Standing Committee
Topic: Reforms in electoral funding
Key recommendations: Enhanced transparency, reduced black money, stronger ECI powers, regulation of political advertising, public disclosure of donor details
Objective: Fairer and more transparent electoral process
mainsPoints: Challenges of electoral funding in India, including lack of transparency and the influence of black money on democratic processes.
Existing legal framework for electoral funding (e.g., Electoral Bonds, provisions of RPA) and their limitations or criticisms.
Arguments for and against various reform measures, such as public financing of elections and stricter disclosure norms.
Role of the Election Commission of India and other constitutional bodies in ensuring free, fair, and ethical elections.
Potential impact of proposed reforms on political parties, voter behavior, and the overall integrity of India's democratic system.
articles.imageDescription: Parliament House of India, symbolizing legislative action.
articles.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body established under Article 324 of the Constitution.

articles.headline: Indian Researchers Announce Breakthrough in Development of Novel Malaria Vaccine Candidate
articles.explainer:
• Scientists at a leading Indian research institution have reported significant progress in a new malaria vaccine candidate.
• The candidate vaccine has shown promising results in pre-clinical trials, demonstrating high efficacy and safety profile.
• Focuses on a novel approach to target the malaria parasite at multiple stages of its life cycle, offering broader protection.
• If successful in further clinical trials, it could offer a crucial tool in global efforts to eradicate malaria.
• Highlights India's growing capabilities in biomedical research, pharmaceutical innovation, and public health contributions.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Discovery: Novel Malaria Vaccine Candidate
Researchers: Indian scientists/institutions
Stage of development: Promising results in pre-clinical trials
Significance: Potential breakthrough for global malaria eradication efforts
mainsPoints: Burden of malaria in India and global health challenges posed by vector-borne diseases.
Importance of vaccine development and indigenous research in combating infectious diseases and ensuring health security.
Role of public and private sector collaboration in accelerating healthcare innovation and drug/vaccine development.
Challenges in vaccine development, including funding, rigorous clinical trials, regulatory approvals, and equitable distribution.
India's potential as a global hub for pharmaceutical manufacturing and vaccine production ('Pharmacy of the World').
articles.imageDescription: Microscopic view of malaria parasites or a scientist working in a lab.
articles.trivia: Malaria is caused by Plasmodium parasites, transmitted through the bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes.

articles.headline: Ministry of Culture Launches New Initiative to Document and Preserve India's Endangered Languages
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture has rolled out a comprehensive program for the protection and promotion of endangered languages.
• Aims to document the rich linguistic diversity of India, including tribal and minor regional languages.
• Involves collaboration with linguistic experts, academic institutions, and local communities for fieldwork.
• Focuses on creating digital archives, dictionaries, grammars, and educational materials in these languages.
• The initiative aligns with UNESCO's efforts to safeguard intangible cultural heritage and promote linguistic diversity globally.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
prelimsPoints: Initiative by: Ministry of Culture, Government of India
Objective: Document and preserve India's endangered languages (tribal, regional)
Methods: Creating digital archives, dictionaries, educational materials; collaboration with experts and communities
Alignment: UNESCO's efforts to safeguard intangible cultural heritage
mainsPoints: Significance of linguistic diversity as an integral part of India's cultural heritage and identity.
Threats faced by endangered languages, including globalization, migration, and lack of intergenerational transmission.
Government's role and various initiatives in promoting and protecting linguistic heritage and tribal cultures.
Challenges in documenting and revitalizing languages, including resource allocation, technological barriers, and community engagement.
Connection between language preservation, cultural identity, traditional knowledge systems, and overall human development.
articles.imageDescription: An ancient manuscript or a group of people from a tribal community.
articles.trivia: The 'People's Linguistic Survey of India' (PLSI) identified over 780 living languages in India.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Scheme to Boost Exports from Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs)
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of MSME launched a new export promotion scheme specifically for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises.
• Provides financial assistance, market access support, and capacity building for MSMEs to tap into global markets.
• Aims to enhance the competitiveness of Indian MSMEs on the international stage by addressing key challenges.
• Focuses on reducing trade barriers, facilitating compliance with international standards, and promoting digital trade.
• Expected to significantly contribute to India's overall export growth, foreign exchange earnings, and job creation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Scheme by: Ministry of MSME
Objective: Boost exports from Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs)
Support mechanisms: Financial assistance, market access support, capacity building
Expected outcomes: Enhanced competitiveness, export growth, job creation
mainsPoints: Role of MSMEs in India's economy, employment generation, and contribution to the export sector.
Challenges faced by MSMEs in accessing international markets, competing globally, and meeting compliance standards.
Effectiveness of government schemes and policy interventions in promoting MSME growth, innovation, and exports.
Strategies for integrating Indian MSMEs into global value chains and leveraging digital platforms for international trade.
Impact of trade policies, free trade agreements, and global economic trends on the performance of the MSME sector.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic symbolizing global trade routes originating from India.
articles.trivia: MSMEs contribute significantly to India's GDP and industrial output, making them a crucial pillar of the economy.

articles.headline: India National Cricket Team Suffers First Home Test Series Defeat to New Zealand
articles.explainer:
• The India national cricket team was defeated in a Test series on home soil for the first time by the New Zealand national cricket team.
• This marks a significant upset in India's strong home record in Test cricket.
• The New Zealand team displayed exceptional performance in all departments to secure the historic win.
• The loss prompts a review of India's strategies and team composition ahead of future international fixtures.
• Cricket enthusiasts and analysts are debating the implications of this rare home series defeat.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
articles.imageDescription: A cricket match in progress with players on the field.
articles.trivia: New Zealand's victory marks a historic moment, as India has been particularly dominant in Test matches played at home.

highlights: Government launches BharatNet Phase III, aiming for universal broadband connectivity via PPP model.
Nine injured in a grenade attack on a Srinagar market; security forces initiate a major search operation.
RBI's MPC maintains status quo on the repo rate, prioritizing inflation management amidst global uncertainties.
India and France deepen strategic ties, focusing on defence cooperation and Indo-Pacific security.
Supreme Court upholds the constitutional validity of 10% EWS reservation, clarifying creamy layer application.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the BharatNet project, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to provide broadband connectivity to all gram panchayats and villages in India.
2. Phase III of the project primarily focuses on a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model.
3. It falls under the ambit of the Ministry of Rural Development.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. BharatNet aims to provide universal broadband connectivity to all gram panchayats and villages. Statement 2 is correct. Phase III of BharatNet emphasizes a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model for faster and more efficient deployment. Statement 3 is incorrect. BharatNet is implemented by Bharat Broadband Network Limited (BBNL), a Special Purpose Vehicle under the Department of Telecommunications (DoT), which falls under the Ministry of Communications, not the Ministry of Rural Development.

prelimsMocks.question:
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is responsible for:
1. Fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate).
2. Achieving the inflation target.
3. Managing government borrowing.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The MPC is mandated to fix the benchmark policy rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target set by the Government of India. Statement 3 is incorrect. Managing government borrowing is primarily a function of the Government of India, though the RBI acts as its debt manager and advises on borrowing strategies, it's not the MPC's direct responsibility.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Ramsar Convention is/are correct?
1. It is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
2. India is a signatory to the Ramsar Convention.
3. A "Montreux Record" is a register of Ramsar sites that need priority conservation attention.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The Ramsar Convention, signed in Ramsar, Iran, in 1971, is indeed an international treaty for wetland conservation. India became a signatory to the Convention in 1982. The Montreux Record is a register of Ramsar sites where changes in ecological character have occurred, are occurring, or are likely to occur as a result of human interference, and thus require urgent conservation measures.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's National Education Policy (NEP) 2020:
1. It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure replacing the 10+2 system.
2. It aims for universalization of Early Childhood Care and Education (ECCE) by 2030.
3. It recommends a single regulator for all higher education institutions, excluding legal and medical education.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. NEP 2020 replaces the 10+2 curricular structure with a new 5+3+3+4 structure. It sets a target for universalization of Early Childhood Care and Education (ECCE) by 2030. It also proposes the Higher Education Commission of India (HECI) as a single overarching umbrella body for all higher education, excluding medical and legal education.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'Critical Information Infrastructure' (CII) is often seen in the news in the context of:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to those information infrastructures, the incapacitation or destruction of which would have a debilitating impact on national security, economy, public health or safety. Therefore, it is directly related to cyber security and national defense, as protecting these assets from cyber threats is crucial.

mainsMocks.question: India's commitment to climate action, as reflected in its enhanced Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs), highlights a delicate balance between developmental aspirations and environmental sustainability. Discuss this statement in the context of India's climate policy and the challenges it faces in achieving its goals.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's enhanced NDCs and the dual challenge of achieving rapid development while addressing climate change. Discuss key components of India's climate action, such as renewable energy targets, emissions intensity reduction, and forest cover goals. Analyze the challenges, including financing green technologies, ensuring energy security for a growing population, technology transfer from developed nations, and the principle of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities. Conclude by outlining India's leadership role and the path forward for sustainable development.

mainsMocks.question: The recent Supreme Court verdict on EWS reservation, while upholding its constitutional validity, raises pertinent questions about the evolving landscape of affirmative action in India. Analyze the implications of this judgment for social justice and the future of reservation policy.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population; Indian Constitution—significant provisions.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by briefly explaining the EWS reservation (103rd Amendment) and the Supreme Court's verdict upholding its validity. Discuss the constitutional basis of reservations in India and how EWS reservation marks a shift from caste-based criteria to economic criteria. Analyze the implications for social justice, including debates around merit vs. equity, the definition of 'vulnerable sections', and the applicability of the 'creamy layer' principle. Explore potential future directions for reservation policy in India, considering economic factors alongside traditional social backwardness.

mainsMocks.question: Robust cyber security architecture for critical infrastructure is paramount for national security and economic stability in the digital age. Examine the challenges involved in securing India's critical information infrastructure and suggest measures to enhance its resilience against sophisticated cyber threats.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, basics of cyber security.
mainsMocks.approach: Define Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) and explain why its protection is vital for national security and economic stability. Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats (state-sponsored attacks, cyber warfare, criminal syndicates) targeting CII. Examine the challenges faced by India in securing its CII, such as technological obsolescence, skill gaps, lack of inter-agency coordination, and supply chain vulnerabilities. Suggest comprehensive measures to enhance resilience, including a 'defense-in-depth' strategy, public-private partnerships, international cooperation, robust legal frameworks, capacity building, and R&D in cyber security.

quickBytes.text: The 'BharatNet' project is implemented by Bharat Broadband Network Limited (BBNL), a Special Purpose Vehicle under DoT.
The 103rd Constitutional Amendment Act (2019) introduced the 10% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS).
The Ramsar Convention was signed in 1971 in Ramsar, Iran, and came into force in 1975.
India is a founding member of BIMSTEC, established in 1997, aiming for regional cooperation in the Bay of Bengal.
The National Education Policy 2020 aims to increase the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education to 50% by 2035.
ISRO's Gaganyaan mission aims to demonstrate human spaceflight capability by sending a crew of three into Low Earth Orbit.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has six members – three from RBI and three external members appointed by the government.
The term 'Indo-Pacific' refers to the geographical region comprising the Indian Ocean and the western and central Pacific Ocean.
The 'Make in India' initiative, launched in 2014, aims to boost the manufacturing sector and job creation in India.
The National Cyber Security Coordinator (NCSC) under the Prime Minister's Office coordinates India's cyber security efforts.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Culture Launches New Initiative to Document and Preserve India's Endangered Languages
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture has rolled out a comprehensive program for the protection and promotion of endangered languages.
• Aims to document the rich linguistic diversity of India, including tribal and minor regional languages.
• Involves collaboration with linguistic experts, academic institutions, and local communities for fieldwork.
• Focuses on creating digital archives, dictionaries, grammars, and educational materials in these languages.
• The initiative aligns with UNESCO's efforts to safeguard intangible cultural heritage and promote linguistic diversity globally.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
prelimsPoints: Initiative by: Ministry of Culture, Government of India
Objective: Document and preserve India's endangered languages (tribal, regional)
Methods: Creating digital archives, dictionaries, educational materials; collaboration with experts and communities
Alignment: UNESCO's efforts to safeguard intangible cultural heritage
mainsPoints: Significance of linguistic diversity as an integral part of India's cultural heritage and identity.
Threats faced by endangered languages, including globalization, migration, and lack of intergenerational transmission.
Government's role and various initiatives in promoting and protecting linguistic heritage and tribal cultures.
Challenges in documenting and revitalizing languages, including resource allocation, technological barriers, and community engagement.
Connection between language preservation, cultural identity, traditional knowledge systems, and overall human development.
GS1.imageDescription: An ancient manuscript or a group of people from a tribal community.
GS1.trivia: The 'People's Linguistic Survey of India' (PLSI) identified over 780 living languages in India.

GS1.headline: India National Cricket Team Suffers First Home Test Series Defeat to New Zealand
GS1.explainer:
• The India national cricket team was defeated in a Test series on home soil for the first time by the New Zealand national cricket team.
• This marks a significant upset in India's strong home record in Test cricket.
• The New Zealand team displayed exceptional performance in all departments to secure the historic win.
• The loss prompts a review of India's strategies and team composition ahead of future international fixtures.
• Cricket enthusiasts and analysts are debating the implications of this rare home series defeat.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
GS1.imageDescription: A cricket match in progress with players on the field.
GS1.trivia: New Zealand's victory marks a historic moment, as India has been particularly dominant in Test matches played at home.

GS2.headline: India and France Conclude Strategic Dialogue, Deepen Cooperation in Defence and Indo-Pacific
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue was held, co-chaired by National Security Advisors of both nations.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defence collaboration, including co-development and co-production of military hardware.
• Both sides reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, emphasizing maritime security.
• Explored avenues for cooperation in counter-terrorism, cyber security, and space technology applications.
• The dialogue underscores the robust and growing strategic partnership between India and France.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Dialogue: India-France Strategic Dialogue
Co-chairs: National Security Advisors (NSAs)
Key areas of cooperation: Defence (co-development, co-production), Indo-Pacific, Cyber Security, Space
Nature of relationship: Robust Strategic Partnership
mainsPoints: Significance of India-France strategic partnership in the evolving global order and multipolar world.
Role of France as a key European partner for India in defence technology transfer and strategic autonomy.
Shared vision for a stable, secure, and prosperous Indo-Pacific region and maritime security challenges.
Challenges and opportunities in expanding bilateral cooperation across various sectors.
Impact of such dialogues on India's geopolitical standing and foreign policy objectives, especially in Europe.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and France displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic partnership with India in 1998.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds EWS Reservation, Clarifies Application of 'Creamy Layer' Principle
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment affirming the constitutional validity of the 10% EWS reservation.
• Also provided clarity on the application of the 'creamy layer' principle within the EWS category, if any, for future policy.
• The verdict addresses long-standing legal challenges to the reservation for Economically Weaker Sections in admissions and employment.
• Emphasizes the state's power under Articles 15(6) and 16(6) to make special provisions for the advancement of any economically weaker section.
• Expected to have significant implications for social justice discourse and public policy on affirmative action.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Judgment by: Supreme Court of India
Topic: Constitutional validity of 10% EWS (Economically Weaker Sections) reservation
Outcome: Upheld the validity of EWS reservation
Additional clarification: Application of 'creamy layer' principle for EWS category
mainsPoints: Constitutional basis and evolution of reservations in India, including the departure with EWS from caste-based criteria.
Arguments for and against EWS reservation, considering principles of equality, meritocracy, and economic disadvantage.
Debate around the 'creamy layer' concept and its applicability across different reservation categories and socio-economic groups.
Impact of the judgment on social equity, access to opportunities in education and employment, and the broader reservation policy.
Role of the judiciary in interpreting constitutional provisions related to affirmative action and balancing competing interests.
GS2.trivia: The 10% EWS reservation was introduced by the 103rd Constitutional Amendment Act in 2019.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Education Conducts High-Level Review of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive meeting was held to assess the progress and challenges in implementing NEP 2020 across states.
• Discussions focused on curriculum reforms, digital education initiatives, and teacher training programs.
• Stakeholders emphasized the need for greater synergy and coordination between central and state governments for effective rollout.
• Identified key areas requiring accelerated action, including early childhood care and education (ECCE) and foundational literacy.
• NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet 21st-century demands and foster holistic development.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
mainsPoints: Salient features and transformative objectives of NEP 2020, including its vision for holistic and multidisciplinary education.
Challenges in implementing large-scale education reforms across a diverse and federal nation, involving multiple stakeholders.
Role of technology in transforming education delivery, access, and learning outcomes, especially in remote areas.
Impact of NEP on skill development, employability, vocational education, and fostering critical thinking among students.
Recommendations for effective coordination between different levels of government and educational institutions for successful implementation.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a classroom engaging with digital learning tools.
GS2.trivia: NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India since independence, following policies in 1968 and 1986.

GS2.headline: India Hosts BIMSTEC Senior Officials' Meeting, Emphasizes Regional Connectivity and Trade
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted the Senior Officials' Meeting (SOM) of the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC).
• Deliberations focused on enhancing regional connectivity, trade facilitation, and people-to-people exchanges.
• Member states discussed progress on various cooperation sectors, including energy, disaster management, and agriculture.
• Reaffirmed commitment to fostering a prosperous, integrated, and secure Bay of Bengal region.
• BIMSTEC is crucial for India's 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies, bridging South and Southeast Asia.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
India and its neighborhood- relations.
prelimsPoints: Meeting: BIMSTEC Senior Officials' Meeting (SOM)
Host: India
Focus areas: Regional connectivity, trade facilitation, energy, disaster management, agriculture
Significance for India: Integration with 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies
mainsPoints: Strategic importance of BIMSTEC for India's regional engagement, economic integration, and geopolitical objectives.
Challenges and opportunities in promoting economic integration and connectivity in the Bay of Bengal region.
Comparison of BIMSTEC with other regional groupings like SAARC, highlighting its unique advantages and focus.
Role of India in driving BIMSTEC's agenda, capacity building, and project implementation.
Potential for BIMSTEC to address common challenges like climate change, disaster management, and maritime security.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of BIMSTEC member countries displayed together.
GS2.trivia: BIMSTEC comprises seven member states: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Standing Committee Recommends Comprehensive Reforms in Electoral Funding
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report on electoral reforms, with a focus on funding transparency.
• Recommended measures to enhance accountability and reduce the influence of black money in elections.
• Suggestions include strengthening the Election Commission's powers and regulating political advertising more strictly.
• Emphasized the need for public disclosure of donor details and stricter enforcement of existing laws.
• Aims to foster a more equitable, transparent, and democratic electoral process in India.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Body: Parliamentary Standing Committee
Topic: Reforms in electoral funding
Key recommendations: Enhanced transparency, reduced black money, stronger ECI powers, regulation of political advertising, public disclosure of donor details
Objective: Fairer and more transparent electoral process
mainsPoints: Challenges of electoral funding in India, including lack of transparency and the influence of black money on democratic processes.
Existing legal framework for electoral funding (e.g., Electoral Bonds, provisions of RPA) and their limitations or criticisms.
Arguments for and against various reform measures, such as public financing of elections and stricter disclosure norms.
Role of the Election Commission of India and other constitutional bodies in ensuring free, fair, and ethical elections.
Potential impact of proposed reforms on political parties, voter behavior, and the overall integrity of India's democratic system.
GS2.imageDescription: Parliament House of India, symbolizing legislative action.
GS2.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body established under Article 324 of the Constitution.

GS3.headline: Government Launches BharatNet Phase III for Universal Broadband Connectivity Across India
GS3.explainer:
• Aims to connect all remaining gram panchayats and villages with high-speed optical fibre internet.
• Utilizes a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model to accelerate deployment and ensure sustainability.
• Expected to boost digital economy, e-governance, telemedicine, and online education in rural areas.
• Builds upon the previous phases of the BharatNet project, addressing last-mile connectivity challenges.
• Focuses on creating an accessible and affordable digital infrastructure backbone for rural India.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment Models
ICT development and its applications
prelimsPoints: BharatNet Phase III launched
Objective: Universal broadband connectivity to gram panchayats and villages
Model: Public-Private Partnership (PPP)
Implementing Ministry: Department of Telecommunications (under Ministry of Communications)
mainsPoints: Significance of digital infrastructure for rural development and economic growth.
Challenges in last-mile connectivity and how the PPP model addresses them.
Impact on e-governance, education, healthcare, and financial inclusion in rural areas.
Role of government initiatives in bridging the digital divide and promoting inclusive growth.
Potential for job creation and local entrepreneurship through enhanced connectivity.
GS3.imageDescription: Optical fibre cables being laid in a rural Indian landscape.
GS3.trivia: The BharatNet project is one of the largest rural broadband projects in the world.

GS3.headline: Nine Injured in Grenade Attack on Srinagar Market; Security Forces Launch Search Operation
GS3.explainer:
• A grenade was lobbed by unknown assailants at a busy market in Srinagar, injuring nine civilians.
• Security forces have cordoned off the area and launched a massive search and cordon operation (CASO).
• The attack comes amidst heightened security arrangements in the region, raising concerns.
• Authorities condemned the act as a cowardly attack and vowed to bring the perpetrators to justice.
• The incident highlights ongoing security challenges and the need for continued vigilance in Jammu & Kashmir.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Location: Srinagar, Jammu & Kashmir
Nature of incident: Grenade attack on a market
Impact: Nine civilians injured
Response: Cordon and Search Operation (CASO) by security forces
mainsPoints: Analysis of the evolving security situation in Jammu & Kashmir and persistent threats.
Challenges faced by security forces in preventing such attacks in civilian-dense areas.
Role of intelligence gathering, community policing, and technology in counter-terrorism efforts.
Impact of such incidents on local populace, tourism, and economic activity in the region.
Government's multi-pronged approach to address militancy, maintain peace, and foster development.
GS3.imageDescription: Security forces personnel cordoning off a market area in Srinagar.
GS3.trivia: Srinagar is historically known for its beautiful Mughal gardens and houseboats on Dal Lake.

GS3.headline: RBI MPC Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate, Reaffirms Focus on Inflation Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its latest meeting.
• Decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level, continuing a cautious monetary policy stance.
• Emphasized vigilance on the inflation trajectory, citing global uncertainties and domestic food price pressures.
• Reiterated commitment to supporting economic growth while primarily ensuring price stability.
• Market analysts anticipate a prolonged pause in rate adjustments, monitoring economic indicators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC meeting outcome: Status quo on repo rate
Primary focus: Inflation management within the target range
Secondary focus: Supporting economic growth
Key monetary policy tool: Repo Rate (rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks)
mainsPoints: Role of RBI's monetary policy in economic stabilization and achieving macro-economic objectives.
Challenges in balancing inflation control and promoting economic growth in a dynamic global environment.
Impact of global economic factors (e.g., crude oil prices, supply chain disruptions) on India's monetary policy decisions.
Effect of an unchanged repo rate on lending, borrowing, investment, and consumer demand.
Significance of the MPC's forward guidance for market expectations and investment climate.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade with its logo.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in India in 2016.

GS3.headline: India Announces Enhanced Emission Reduction Targets Ahead of COP29, Reaffirms Climate Leadership
GS3.explainer:
• India declared updated Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) with more ambitious emission reduction goals.
• The announcement comes in preparation for the upcoming COP29 summit, showcasing India's commitment to global climate action.
• New targets include increased renewable energy capacity and further reductions in emissions intensity of its economy.
• Emphasizes the principle of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC).
• Aims to balance rapid economic development with environmental sustainability, promoting green growth strategies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: India's new commitment: Enhanced Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs)
Context: Ahead of COP29 (Conference of Parties 29)
Key aspects: Increased renewable energy targets, further reduced emissions intensity
Principle upheld: Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
mainsPoints: India's evolving stance on climate change and its role as a responsible global actor and a voice for developing nations.
Challenges and opportunities in achieving ambitious emission targets while ensuring energy security and economic growth.
Significance of technology transfer, climate finance, and capacity building for developing nations like India.
Impact of climate policies and green growth strategies on various sectors of the Indian economy.
Critique of global climate governance and the imperative for equitable burden sharing among nations.
GS3.imageDescription: Solar panels against a blue sky, symbolizing renewable energy.
GS3.trivia: COP29 is scheduled to be held in Baku, Azerbaijan.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Advanced Propulsion System for Gaganyaan Human Spaceflight Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) conducted a critical test of an advanced propulsion system.
• The system is designed for the service module of the Gaganyaan mission, India's maiden human spaceflight program.
• The test validated its performance, reliability, and re-ignition capabilities in simulated space conditions.
• Marks a significant milestone in ensuring the safety and success of the crewed mission's orbital phase.
• Further strengthens India's capabilities in independent human spaceflight endeavors and advanced rocketry.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Agency: Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)
Mission: Gaganyaan (India's first human spaceflight mission)
Test: Advanced Propulsion System for the Service Module
Significance: Critical step towards achieving indigenous human spaceflight capability
mainsPoints: Strategic importance of India's human spaceflight program (Gaganyaan) for national prestige and technological advancement.
Technological challenges involved in developing advanced propulsion systems for crewed missions and ensuring astronaut safety.
Potential spin-off benefits of space technology for civilian applications and economic development.
India's position in the global space race and potential for international collaboration in human space exploration.
Economic and scientific impact of indigenous space capabilities on research, innovation, and job creation.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of the Gaganyaan crew module in orbit.
GS3.trivia: The Gaganyaan mission aims to send a three-member crew to an orbit of 400 km for a 3-day mission.

GS3.headline: Economic Survey Projects Robust Q2 GDP Growth, Manufacturing Sector Shows Strong Recovery
GS3.explainer:
• Latest economic survey data indicates a strong rebound in India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) for the second quarter.
• The manufacturing sector, in particular, has shown significant resilience and growth, driving overall economic expansion.
• Positive sentiment attributed to government's structural reforms, robust domestic demand, and investment push.
• Challenges remain from global headwinds, geopolitical uncertainties, and inflationary pressures, requiring continued policy vigilance.
• Projections suggest India is on track to be one of the fastest-growing major economies globally, sustaining momentum.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Q2 GDP growth projected as robust by economic survey
Key driver for growth: Manufacturing sector showing strong recovery
Contributing factors: Government's structural reforms, strong domestic demand
Lingering challenges: Global headwinds, inflationary pressures
mainsPoints: Analysis of factors contributing to India's economic growth momentum post-pandemic and global shocks.
Role of the manufacturing sector in job creation, value addition, and achieving 'Make in India' objectives.
Government policies and initiatives (e.g., PLI schemes) aimed at boosting industrial output and investment.
Impact of global economic slowdowns, supply chain disruptions, and geopolitical tensions on India's growth trajectory.
Strategies for achieving sustainable, inclusive, and resilient economic growth in the long term.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic showing an upward trending economic growth chart.
GS3.trivia: India's economy is often characterized by its large domestic market and youthful population.

GS3.headline: Government Issues New Guidelines to Strengthen Cyber Security Framework for Critical Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released updated guidelines for critical infrastructure sectors.
• Aims to enhance the resilience of sectors like energy, finance, transport, and communication against cyber threats.
• Mandates regular security audits, threat intelligence sharing, and establishment of dedicated cyber security cells.
• Focuses on a 'defense-in-depth' strategy to protect essential services from sophisticated cyber attacks and espionage.
• Part of India's broader national cyber security strategy to safeguard digital assets and ensure national security.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Issuing authority for guidelines: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
Purpose: Strengthen cyber security for Critical Information Infrastructure (CII)
Key measures mandated: Regular security audits, threat intelligence sharing, dedicated cyber security cells
Strategy adopted: 'Defense-in-depth' approach
mainsPoints: Growing threat of cyber attacks on critical infrastructure and its implications for national security and economic stability.
Challenges in securing diverse, interconnected, and often legacy critical systems across various sectors.
Government's multi-stakeholder approach to cyber security governance, involving public and private entities.
Role of international cooperation and information sharing in combating trans-national cyber threats and state-sponsored attacks.
Balancing security imperatives with innovation, technological advancement, and individual privacy in the digital domain.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital representation of interconnected networks and security shields.
GS3.trivia: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.

GS3.headline: Government Declares New Ramsar Sites, Bolstering India's Wetland Conservation Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• Several new wetlands across India have been designated as Ramsar Sites, significantly increasing the total count.
• This declaration signifies their international importance for biodiversity, ecological functions, and hydrological services.
• The move underscores India's commitment to the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands of International Importance.
• Aims to promote conservation and wise use of these critical ecosystems, which are vital for environmental health.
• Wetlands play a crucial role in water purification, flood control, carbon sequestration, and supporting diverse flora and fauna.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Environmental conservation; national parks, wildlife sanctuaries, biosphere reserves, tiger reserves, Ramsar sites, etc.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Declaration: New Ramsar Sites in India
Purpose: Enhanced wetland conservation and recognition of international importance
International convention involved: Ramsar Convention on Wetlands
Role of wetlands: Water purification, flood control, carbon sequestration, biodiversity support
mainsPoints: Ecological significance of wetlands, including their role as biodiversity hotspots and providers of ecosystem services.
Objectives and implementation of the Ramsar Convention in India, and the process of designating Ramsar Sites.
Challenges in managing and protecting Ramsar Sites, including anthropogenic pressures, pollution, and climate change impacts.
Government initiatives and policies for wetland conservation beyond Ramsar designation, such as the National Wetland Conservation Programme.
Role of local communities, scientific research, and public awareness in effective wetland management and sustainable use.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds.
GS3.trivia: The Ramsar Convention is the only global treaty focused on a single ecosystem type – wetlands.

GS3.headline: Indian Researchers Announce Breakthrough in Development of Novel Malaria Vaccine Candidate
GS3.explainer:
• Scientists at a leading Indian research institution have reported significant progress in a new malaria vaccine candidate.
• The candidate vaccine has shown promising results in pre-clinical trials, demonstrating high efficacy and safety profile.
• Focuses on a novel approach to target the malaria parasite at multiple stages of its life cycle, offering broader protection.
• If successful in further clinical trials, it could offer a crucial tool in global efforts to eradicate malaria.
• Highlights India's growing capabilities in biomedical research, pharmaceutical innovation, and public health contributions.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Discovery: Novel Malaria Vaccine Candidate
Researchers: Indian scientists/institutions
Stage of development: Promising results in pre-clinical trials
Significance: Potential breakthrough for global malaria eradication efforts
mainsPoints: Burden of malaria in India and global health challenges posed by vector-borne diseases.
Importance of vaccine development and indigenous research in combating infectious diseases and ensuring health security.
Role of public and private sector collaboration in accelerating healthcare innovation and drug/vaccine development.
Challenges in vaccine development, including funding, rigorous clinical trials, regulatory approvals, and equitable distribution.
India's potential as a global hub for pharmaceutical manufacturing and vaccine production ('Pharmacy of the World').
GS3.imageDescription: Microscopic view of malaria parasites or a scientist working in a lab.
GS3.trivia: Malaria is caused by Plasmodium parasites, transmitted through the bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Scheme to Boost Exports from Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs)
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of MSME launched a new export promotion scheme specifically for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises.
• Provides financial assistance, market access support, and capacity building for MSMEs to tap into global markets.
• Aims to enhance the competitiveness of Indian MSMEs on the international stage by addressing key challenges.
• Focuses on reducing trade barriers, facilitating compliance with international standards, and promoting digital trade.
• Expected to significantly contribute to India's overall export growth, foreign exchange earnings, and job creation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Scheme by: Ministry of MSME
Objective: Boost exports from Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs)
Support mechanisms: Financial assistance, market access support, capacity building
Expected outcomes: Enhanced competitiveness, export growth, job creation
mainsPoints: Role of MSMEs in India's economy, employment generation, and contribution to the export sector.
Challenges faced by MSMEs in accessing international markets, competing globally, and meeting compliance standards.
Effectiveness of government schemes and policy interventions in promoting MSME growth, innovation, and exports.
Strategies for integrating Indian MSMEs into global value chains and leveraging digital platforms for international trade.
Impact of trade policies, free trade agreements, and global economic trends on the performance of the MSME sector.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic symbolizing global trade routes originating from India.
GS3.trivia: MSMEs contribute significantly to India's GDP and industrial output, making them a crucial pillar of the economy.

sources: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
